channel.title: Craft
channel.description: Craft is a list of hand oriented craft and technology Do we really need all those power tools?

item.title: Now Shipping: ‘Thomas Jefferson’s Declaration Desk’
item.description: Now available in our store is Ed Zappen’s book “Thomas Jefferson’s Declaration Desk.” Inside, you’ll find construction drawings, details and techniques, as well as plenty of color photos, to guide you as you build your own faithful replica of the Declaration Desk. Plus, an overview of events that led to the American Revolution and some...
item.source:markdown: Now available in our store is Ed Zappen’s book “Thomas Jefferson’s Declaration Desk.” Inside, you’ll find construction drawings, details and techniques, as well as plenty of color photos, to guide you as you build your own faithful replica of the Declaration Desk. Plus, an overview of events that led to the American Revolution and some...

item.title: 112. Otto D'Ambrosio
item.description:
For episode 112 of the podcast, I'm talking with Otto D'Ambrosio.

Otto wears two hats—as a solo luthier and Chief Designer at Eastman Guitars—and he walks us through his journey from working with legends like Flip Scipio, John Monteleone, and Carl Thompson to his role today, bridging the gap between hands-on building and production design.
 
We talk about how the pandemic pushed him into 3D CAD, changing the way he collaborates with Eastman's workshops, and dive into some of his design work—including the Fullertone neck system, his boutique archtop builds, and an upcoming project comparing domestic and European tonewoods.

Links:
https://dambrosioguitars.com/

Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of Bashkin Guitars and brought to you by the Fretboard Journal. This episode is sponsored by the Looth Group, Dream Guitars and StewMac.

Michael Bashkin's Hub of Acoustics 2026 US Academy: https://hubofacoustics.com/en/#Colorado_Academy
item.source:markdown:
For episode 112 of the podcast, I'm talking with Otto D'Ambrosio.
Otto wears two hats—as a solo luthier and Chief Designer at Eastman Guitars—and he walks us through his journey from working with legends like Flip Scipio, John Monteleone, and Carl Thompson to his role today, bridging the gap between hands-on building and production design.
We talk about how the pandemic pushed him into 3D CAD, changing the way he collaborates with Eastman's workshops, and dive into some of his design work—including the Fullertone neck system, his boutique archtop builds, and an upcoming project comparing domestic and European tonewoods.
Links:
[https://dambrosioguitars.com/](https://dambrosioguitars.com/)
Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of [Bashkin Guitars](https://www.bashkinguitars.com) and brought to you by the [Fretboard Journal](https://shop.fretboardjournal.com/products/fretboard-journal-annual-subscription). This episode is sponsored by [the Looth Group](https://loothgroup.com), [Dream Guitars](https://www.dreamguitars.com/) and [StewMac](https://www.stewmac.com/?irclickid=VA-TmuXZ%3AxyPUn0Ut-05ZTupUkHUPAzGE2bmy00&utm_source=3755630&utm_medium=Impact&utm_campaign=3755630&utm_content=Online%20Tracking%20Link_1303370&irgwc=1&partner=Fretboard%20Journal&mpid=3755630&group=).
Michael Bashkin's Hub of Acoustics 2026 US Academy: [https://hubofacoustics.com/en/#Colorado\_Academy](https://hubofacoustics.com/en/#Colorado_Academy)

item.description:

item.title: Mind Upon Mind: Lunch Tray Sandwich
item.description: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...
item.source:markdown: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/shopnotes-podcast-episode-266-overfeatured/)

item.title: New Model Mandolin: 2
item.description:
I would have bent my first set of sides during my first year at the London College of Furniture (1976/77) and I’ve bent a fair few bits of wood since. It never ceases to amaze me what you can do with a drop of moisture and a bending iron. I wasn’t 100% sure about the extreme bending that this new shape would need but as you’ll see (spoiler alert) it all went well. So, in part two, I make the rim of the mandolin: bend the sides, fit various blocks, linings etc and use my radius dish for fitting the back. Also, I get the neck blank made too! Quite a lot to take in, so get yourself a cup of tea.

Cheers Gary
item.source:markdown:
I would have bent my first set of sides during my first year at the London College of Furniture (1976/77) and I’ve bent a fair few bits of wood since. It never ceases to amaze me what you can do with a drop of moisture and a bending iron. I wasn’t 100% sure about the extreme bending that this new shape would need but as you’ll see (spoiler alert) it all went well. So, in part two, I make the rim of the mandolin: bend the sides, fit various blocks, linings etc and use my radius dish for fitting the back. Also, I get the neck blank made too! Quite a lot to take in, so get yourself a cup of tea.
Cheers Gary
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)

item.title: ‘17th-Century Essex County Cupboard’ to Prepress
item.description: I uploaded the interior files to our printer on Tuesday for Peter Follansbee’s latest book, “17th-Century Essex County Cupboard” – so by the time you read this, I hope we’re finalizing the cover. This one is a tour de force – a twice-in-a-lifetime project (though I’ll bet Peter wouldn’t mind making it again) that we...
item.source:markdown: I uploaded the interior files to our printer on Tuesday for Peter Follansbee’s latest book, “17th-Century Essex County Cupboard” – so by the time you read this, I hope we’re finalizing the cover. item.title: New (More) Storefront Hours
item.description: Good news – we’re adding Tuesday hours to the storefront. Starting on March 24, the Lost Art Press store (407 Madison Ave., Covington, Ky., 40411) will be open from 10 a.m.-2 pm. every Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and Friday. Kara Gebhart Uhl, author of “Cadi & the Cursed Oak” and Lost Art Press copy editor (among...
item.source:markdown: Good news – we’re adding Tuesday hours to the storefront. item.title: 18th-century Tools for Every Shop

item.title: New Model Mandolin: 1
item.description:
No sooner one mandolin is complete than another comes along!

So, after a very long gestation period, my New Model Mandolin is finally being realised. In this first episode of a new build series of videos, I look at its overall shape and go into detail about the making of the external mould. The mould is used for accurate construction of the mandolin’s rim.
item.source:markdown:
No sooner one mandolin is complete than another comes along!
So, after a very long gestation period, my New Model Mandolin is finally being realised. In this first episode of a new build series of videos, I look at its overall shape and go into detail about the making of the external mould. The mould is used for accurate construction of the mandolin’s rim.
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)

item.title: Simple Dovetailed Step Stool
item.description: This four-piece dovetailed step stool is a weekend class I sometimes teach – but unlike for most of my classes, it’s not a project I, er, borrowed from Chris or wrote about myself for a magazine article or book. It’s an excellent beginner’s dovetailing project, and you end up with a nice little stool that’s...

item.title: Golf Tee Finishing Stands
item.description:
When I’m finishing a project, I support it on small stands. They raise the project off the bench so I can get at its lower edges. I’ve seen stands like...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/10/aw_14821_008-1024x683.jpg)When I’m finishing a project, I support it on small stands. They raise the project off the bench so I can get at its lower edges. I’ve seen stands like...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/golf-tee-finishing-stands/)
